issue_title: data cacher: introduce more efficient timestamp verifications
issue_body:
Originally on 2011-08-08

Several data cachers use `get_table_update_time()` in order to check whether data has changed in DB and so whether they have to reload the cache.  `get_table_update_time()` is implemented via, basically, `SHOW TABLE STATUS` SQL command.  Testing showed that such a timestamp verifier is actually rather time consuming and so may be problematic when an Invenio installation is heavily bombarded by many concurrent user requests.

For example, here is an old October 2010 comparison:

```
In [3]: %timeit x = run_sql("SHOW TABLE STATUS LIKE 'idxINDEX';")
1000 loops, best of 3: 1.8 ms per loop

In [4]: %timeit y = run_sql("SELECT MAX(last_updated) FROM idxINDEX;")
1000 loops, best of 3: 217 µs per loop

In [5]: %timeit z = run_sql("SELECT UPDATE_TIME FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.TABLES WHERE table_name='idxINDEX';")
100 loops, best of 3: 2.08 ms per loop
```

It is therefore interesting to amend existing data cachers to replace `get_table_update_time()` technique by a simpler, faster SELECT SQL statements in existing data cacher definitions, whenever possible.

1) Sometimes we may be able to use some existing timestamp columns sometimes, such as in the fictive example using `idxINDEX.last_updated` above.

2) When not, then we can introduce new timestamp columns in existing tables.  Note that this could sometimes lead to inefficient repetition of column values, e.g. when we would like to add `last_updated` to all the rows of the `collection` table, provided that webcoll generates only single timestamp for all collections anyway.

3) If it is not possible to transform definitions into effective SQL queries with the current definitions and/or after addition of columns as described in the previous two techniques, for example because we would have to study many tables in order to detect the final timestamp value, then it is will be good to have a generic data cache table that would hold timestamps for the most performance critical data cachers. The table may look like this:

```
  datacacher
  ---------
  id
  name
  definition
  timestamp
```

The data cachers in the application would then run the following simple SQL statement:

```
   SELECT timestamp FROM datacacher WHERE name='collection_webpage';
```

in order to get the latest DB timestamp so as to verify whether the local collection webpage cache is to be reloaded or not.

Note that this requires a new data cacher timestamp gatherer daemon to be running in the background periodically.  This daemon would be permanently scanning data cacher definitions and filling datacacher.timestamp column values accordingly.  For example, the collection webpage data cacher (currently used in webcoll only, but see Carmen's forthcoming branch) uses the following tables to collect its timestamp:

```
websearch_webcoll.py: database_tables_timestamps.append(get_table_update_time('bibrec'))
websearch_webcoll.py: database_tables_timestamps.append(get_table_update_time('bibfmt'))
websearch_webcoll.py: database_tables_timestamps.append(get_table_update_time('idxWORD%'))
websearch_webcoll.py: database_tables_timestamps.append(get_table_update_time('collection%'))
websearch_webcoll.py: database_tables_timestamps.append(get_table_update_time('portalbox'))
websearch_webcoll.py: database_tables_timestamps.append(get_table_update_time('field%'))
websearch_webcoll.py: database_tables_timestamps.append(get_table_update_time('format%'))
websearch_webcoll.py: database_tables_timestamps.append(get_table_update_time('rnkMETHODNAME'))
```

Therefore, in order to populate the datacacher table, statements like these are to be executed on the background periodically.

4) Usually the situation is not that convoluted; most data cachers consult one-two tables only, so for these data cachers the technique number 3 would not be necessary; it should be sufficient to use techniques 1 and 2 in these cases.  However, having a generic store for any future data cachers would be nice to have anyway.  It is a part of this ticket to verify how many cases of 1, 2 vs 3 there would be in order to see the importance and the spread of a generic technique like 3.

5) Note that the datacacher table would be ultra small, holding mostly the list of registered data cachers and their DB time stamps, together with their definitions and anything suitable for running of the date cacher timestamp gatherer daemon, but nothing much else.  It would usually not hold any other runtime data, these are usually stored elsewhere, say in the citation tables in case of the citation daemon.

(Just some (older) musings in writing before we re-discuss IRL.)

issue_title: BTrees setup.py doesn't respect user defined index-url setting, breaking pip and requirements.txt style installs
issue_body:
When installing BTrees, the index-url of all dependencies seem to be "forced" to pypi.python.org regardless of the index-url setting in requirements.txt or in ~/.pip.ini

This creates serious problems in environments where Artifactory is used as a "proxy" to pypi.python.org and where direct connectivity to pypi.python.org is not permitted by policy. This causes the install to hang, and then finally fail with an error saying it failed to retrieve a pypi.python.org URL.

This is easy to reproduce if you have an Artifactory server or some other non pypi.python.org PyPi repository URL configured in your ~/.pip.ini like this:

--- snip ---
[global]
index-url = http://my.local.repo/artifactory/api/pypi/pypi-repos/simple
--- snip ---

When I do pip install BTrees, the first HTTP/HTTPS request is made to grab the BTrees package from my local Artifactory server, as expected. After that, the 'persistent' package dependency is fetched since it is a dependency of BTrees- but it is not fetched from the Artifactory server as one would expect- instead, it always goes directly to pypi.python.org to try to fetch the package.

The workaround is to manually install all of the dependencies before install BTrees, so that it doesn't need to fetch any dependencies.

I would like to submit a pull request to fix the issue, but I'm not terribly familiar with setuptools or pip. Maybe this is an easy fix for someone who knows the tools better?

Thanks.

issue_title: Check for None in __doc__ before changing it.
issue_body:
Python code byte-compiled with -OO has doc-strings stripped out.

This creates problems when compiling different packages which changes the doc-strings by doing something like this:
doc += "additional text"
(when the docstring is 'None', this will fail).

mgcv_cubic_splines.py does this and thus creates problems when trying to bytecompile it with the -OO option using cx_freeze/py2exe.

In order to fix this, I would propose to change all the lines  which say:
__doc__ += CubicRegressionSpline.common_doc 
with
__doc__ =__doc__ +CubicRegressionSpline.common_doc if __doc__ else ''

See also:
http://bugs.python.org/issue23189#
http://stackoverflow.com/questions/22299532/unsupported-operand-types-for-nonetype-and-str-winappdbg-error-after-c

issue_title: If one binding has already been imported, then qtpy should just use it
issue_body:
Consider, for example, a system where solely pyqt4 and pyqt5 are installed.  If one does
```
import PyQt5.QtGui
import qtpy
```
this fails with
```
qtpy.PythonQtError: No Qt bindings could be found
```
(even though obviously there is a Qt binding available) because PyQt4.QtGui cannot be imported at all after PyQt5.QtGui has already been imported.

Fortunately there is an easy solution, which is to check, before doing anything else, whether PyQt4/5/PySide/2 are already present in sys.modules, and, if one of them is, just use it.

Real use case:
matplotlib (which can use any of the bindings) is considering switching from using its own qt shim to qtpy.  However, it is not rare that one user specifies a given binding to use (via the matplotlibrc backend param), but sometimes wants to use some GUI program that is explicitly written (solely) against PyQt5.  Then starting the GUI will import PyQt5 and matplotlib would fail to initialize its backend if it did not first check whether a given binding had already been forced for the process.

issue_title: Add support for proxying
Refresh adb connection to a device at the end of AndroidDevice teardown.
issue_body:
It has several benefits:
1. Kills any lingering adb processes/cmds that the test may have started and does not clean up properly.
2. Releases all forwarded adb ports.
3. Recovers unresponsive devices in some cases. Some devices may stop responding to all adb commands due to adb calls made by tests, leaving the device in an unusable state. Refreshing adb connection fixes the problem.

Overall this seems to be a good approach since it can clears any baggage a test may have left with adb and makes the execution cleaner.

issue_title: Don't assume __STDC_VERSION__ is defined
issue_body:
See https://travis-ci.org/quixdb/squash/jobs/148061907#L1491

Note that this is coming from types.h, so it happens when compiling code *using* brotli, not necessarily in brotli itself.  Even if you don't care about `-Wundef` in brotli, IMHO it should be possible for people who just want to use brotli to use whatever warning flags they want.

Untested, but this should do the trick:

```diff
diff --git a/common/types.h b/common/types.h
index 4b050af..3508d74 100644
--- a/common/types.h
+++ b/common/types.h
@@ -25,7 +25,7 @@ typedef __int64 int64_t;
 #endif  /* defined(_MSC_VER) && (_MSC_VER < 1600) */
 
 #if (!defined(_MSC_VER) || (_MSC_VER >= 1800)) && \
-    (defined(__cplusplus) || __STDC_VERSION__ >= 199901L)
+    (defined(__cplusplus) || (defined(__STDC_VERSION__) && __STDC_VERSION__ >= 199901L))
 #include <stdbool.h>
 #define BROTLI_BOOL bool
 #define BROTLI_TRUE true
```

issue_title: cutout_row/cutout_col for MEDS output type when using offsets.
issue_body:
I believe the cutout_row and cutout_col columns in the OBJECT_DATA extension of a MEDS file should give the (zero-offset) position of the object in pixel coordinates (@esheldon can confirm).  Currently this is set to the origin of the stamp wcs in WriteMeds in des_meds.py (called 'row0' and 'col0' in des_meds.py, see e.g. line 263). 

I think this behavior will become problematic as soon as you have more than one exposure of an object with different offsets. I think the solution is to have MEDSBuilder access the stamp offsets (I think this could be done with an extra output type, but maybe there is an easier way?), and make these an attribute of MultiExposureObject. Then WriteMEDS would also be able to access this info. I can go ahead and try to implement this, but keen to hear if @rmjarvis @esheldon or others have thoughts on this before I do so.

issue_title: More WFIRST Updates
issue_body:
Some updates that Rachel didn't get to in branch #675.  I'm making a new issue for these to refocus the effort to this list of remaining tasks:

* Updating Zernikes since cycle 5
* Incorporating their variation across the SCAs
* Any other changes in cycle 7 besides the ones listed above as going into this PR!
* Make WFIRST module components accessible in config
* Dither sequences (re-think connection to Chris's code)
* Simpler persistence specifications
* Brighter-fatter: empirically-based Antilogus-type implementation (CDModel) using coefficients measured by Chris
* Correlated read noise: should store a correlation function in WFIRST module and use it to generate the read noise. [no new coding needed]
* Individual pixel effects: ability to put in a flat field including effects down to the sub-pixel level. Cross-hatching pattern could then be simulated.
* Defects like cosmic rays
* Re-enable WCS tests that were removed because we no longer have a sample WCS from the WFIRST project office.

People interested in doing a subset of these should feel free to spin off separate issues as appropriate.

issue_title: duplicate point removal isn't really optional
issue_body:
The removal of duplicate points in HDF5 files is only optional deep inside the implementation code. It should be optional at high level. It isn't obvious how to implement this, but we could give the neuron loading functions some optional keyword arguments that are passed on to the readers.

A related issue is that point removal should be applicable to all file types, not just HDF5. I will open a separate issue for that.

issue_title: Use of font-awesome icons in desktop applications / Standard Icon naming
issue_body:
Opening an issue here in case someone is interested in using Font Awesome icons in desktop applications, they might want to have a look at [qtawesome](https://github.com/spyder-ide/qtawesome), which allows to use font awesome glyphs as standard QIcons in PyQt and PySide applications. 

```python
import qtawesome as qta
fa_icon = qta.icon('fa.flag')
fa_button = QtGui.QPushButton(fa_icon, 'Font Awesome!')
```
We support styling, stacking icons, and animations. Our primary motivation is to use Font Awesome for the icon set of the [Spyder IDE](https://github.com/spyder-ide/spyder) (Spyder is a desktop IDE for the Python programming language). 
As we are working on the transition to font-awesome, we naturally missed a few icons, and I create a short list of glyphs to fill the gap. I know that you don't take PRs with icons, but I would be glad to contribute them, should you be interested.

Regarding the naming of icons, it could be interesting in the context of using font-awesome for desktop applications to either create aliases following the naming conventions from the [FreeDesktop specifications](http://freedesktop.org/wiki/Specifications/icon-naming-spec) or to create mapping to names following the freedesktop specifications. This could help defining creating standard themes for Qt / KDE based on Font Awesome.

issue_title: Icons are misplaced in contexts with non-zero text-indent values
issue_body:
Using Font Awesome version 4.5.0 and Chrome 49.0.2623.110 (64-bit).

FA uses display: inline-block for icons, but this inherits text-indent values from enclosing tags.

See http://jsfiddle.net/crisperdue/1o0bbhgc/4/ for illustration and a workaround.

My suggestion is to include "text-indent: 0" in the definition of the "fa" class to positively set the text-indent for icons to 0.

issue_title: Callback for custom operations after initial bootstrap
issue_body:
Our usecase involves creating a lot of database clusters that need to have some initial database setup (some specific databases with extensions) created on the cluster (so, only once after the initial initdb bootstrapping) before becoming usable.

So we would like to have a way for Patroni to invoke the running of these tasks after the db is created but before the node is deemed ready. 

I was thinking on adding a new `on_bootstrap` hook that gets executed after the initial bootstrap currently done [here](https://github.com/zalando/patroni/blob/master/patroni/postgresql.py#L973) and blocks until completion. 

Would this make sense?, can you think of any other way to achieve this?

issue_title: Patroni can set initialize flag and lost ability to bootstrap.
issue_body:
We have problems with our etcd installation. Because of that we encounter bug concerning initialize key creation in method `patroni.ha.Ha#bootstrap` . It tries to create new key `if self.dcs.initialize(create_new=True):` but fails many times because of ReadTimeoutError exception. But one of requests actually hit etcd and was successful. In next iteration patroni saw empty key instead of None and starts to wait master to bootsrap. When we found cluster in this state we check etcd data and found initialize key with empty value. 

This bug should not be an issue in normal environment but still can happen.

Possible solution will be to create initialize key (or another key) with member name and proceed with initialization if initialize value equals member name. 

Patroni logs:

issue_title: Add restore from backup support
issue_body:
A feature request I got: when restoring a cluster from backup one has to make sure all Patroni instances are shut down before replacing one data dir from backup, doing the whole recovery dance and possibly adjusting `/optime/leader` and then bringing the nodes back up in correct order. Would be nice to have some tooling for that to reduce room for operator errors and speed things up. Ideally make a restore from backup scriptable.

Something along the lines of:

1. patronictl backup-recovery init - shut down postgresql on all nodes.
2a. patronictl backup-recovery complete - start postgres, make it the leader, update /optime/leader, reinit standby's.
2b. patronictl backup-recovery abort - start rest of the cluster, reinit current node.

I'll leave this as a place holder until the idea has had some time to develop.

issue_body:
To enable easy monitoring it would be helpful to have an api endpoint providing a healthcheck for the cluster.

We should include:

issue_title: classical baval app. teo bugs
issue_body:
Classical naval app is in my /usr/bin/naval file:
```
#!/usr/bin/python3

"""Naval Fate.

Usage:
  naval_fate.py ship new <name>...
  naval_fate.py ship <name> move <x> <y> [--speed=<kn>]
  naval_fate.py ship shoot <x> <y>
  naval_fate.py mine (set|remove) <x> <y> [--moored | --drifting]
  naval_fate.py (-h | --help)
  naval_fate.py --version

Options:
  -h --help     Show this screen.
  --version     Show version.
  --speed=<kn>  Speed in knots [default: 10].
  --moored      Moored (anchored) mine.
  --drifting    Drifting mine.

"""
from docopt import docopt


if __name__ == '__main__':
    arguments = docopt(__doc__, version='Naval Fate 2.0')
    print(arguments)

```

Generate autocomletion for that via `docopt-completion naval` and have 2 bugs:

1. commands completes with variety of files in current dir except naval commands. Looking in bashcompletion file gave me that:
`COMPREPLY=( $( compgen -fW ' move shoot new' -- $cur) )`

it should be just `compgen -W`, not `-fW`. `f` id sor files, i suppose.

2. pressing tab after `naval mine set ..pressing tab..` gives me nothing or filnames in current dir. But it should print me `--moored --drifting` and probably `<x> <y>`.

If i type `naval mine set 10 10 ..press tab..` it gives me nothing neither.

issue_title: Un-fork this forked repo
issue_body:
It appears we're in a situation where a fork (irods/python-irodsclient) has now become "upstream", and the original upstream repo (iplantcollaborativeopensource/python-irodsclient) is no longer maintained. Aside from being unintuitive, this is a usability issue as github.com doesn't let users search the code inside a fork.

I suggest the maintainers do something [like this](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/29326767/unfork-a-github-fork-without-deleting). Also, perhaps iplantcollaborativeopensource/python-irodsclient should be deprecated if it's no longer maintained; I can help with this on our end.

issue_title: Abattement / Neutralisation des ressources dans le cadre de l'ASS
issue_body:
Suite à un retour utilisateur sur l'ASS, je me pose des questions sur la formule de calcul de l'ASS pour une personne seule et dans un couple.

En particulier, je ne comprends pas pourquoi l'abattement et la neutralisation des ressources précédentes est gérée différemment entre le demandeur et le conjoint pour calculer `ass_base_ressources`.

Pour le demandeur https://fr.openfisca.org/legislation/ass_base_ressources_individu , il s'agit d'une neutralisation totale des précédents salaires si le salaire du dernier mois est à 0 :

```
    salaire_imposable_interrompu = (salaire_imposable > 0) * (salaire_imposable_this_month == 0)
    # Le Salaire d'une activité partielle est neutralisé en cas d'interruption
    salaire_imposable = (1 - salaire_imposable_interrompu) * salaire_imposable
```

Pour le conjoint, https://fr.openfisca.org/legislation/ass_base_ressources_conjoint , l'abattement est de 100 ou de 30%, en se basant sur https://www.legifrance.gouv.fr/affichCodeArticle.do?idArticle=LEGIARTI000020398006&cidTexte=LEGITEXT000006072050 . Deux variables spécifiques sans contreparties pour l'individu sont même disponibles :

```
        tx_abat_partiel = parameters(period).prestations.minima_sociaux.ass.abat_rev_subst_conj
        tx_abat_total = parameters(period).prestations.minima_sociaux.ass.abat_rev_non_subst_conj
```

Je ne trouve pas de trace de cette distinction d'abattement entre le conjoint et le demandeur dans les textes.

Pour aller plus loin, pour le conjoint, trois autres ressources sont prises en compte : la retraite nette, le chômage net et les indemnités de stage alors que ces ressources sont normalement exclues de l'ASS. La formule de calcul appliqué devrait normalement neutraliser le chômage et les indemnités de stage ; cependant, après avoir tracé à la main la formule de la neutralisation (avec le paramètre à true), cette neutralisation n'intervient que si elles sont interrompues, ce qui va à l'encontre de ce que j'ai compris de l'ASS (l'allocation chomage et les revenus des stages sont exclus de la base ressource : https://www.service-public.fr/particuliers/vosdroits/F12484 ).

- Est ce que quelqu'un peut m'aider à y voir plus clair et me corriger dans la compréhension de l'ASS et de son interprétation par OpenFisca ?

Merci !

_Cette issue a peut-être un lien avec https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-france/issues/932_

issue_title: Documenter qu'il faut tester systématiquement les changements
issue_body:
Les tests d'openfisca-france sont un peu disparates : certaines prestations sont très bien testées (notamment grâce aux tests mes aides), d'autres ne le sont pas du tout.

Des choses telles que [PR#384](https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-france/pull/384) ou [Issue#385](https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-france/issues/385)/[Issue#386](https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-france/issues/386) ne devraient pas être possible.

Il est peut être nécessaire de définir une stratégie de test systématique, voici quelques points qui pourraient améliorer les choses ? :
- Faire un script qui vérifie qu'une formule est testée d'une manière ou d'une autre pour une valeur différente de zéro.

Certaines formules sont testés de fait car une autre formule appelante est testée, il faudrait s'assurer que 1) toutes les formules sont testées ; 2) Qu'elles sont toutes testées pour une valeur différente de zéro.
- Imposer que toute nouvelle prestation codée fasse l'objet d'un test avec la pull request qui l'accompagne. Ça permettrait d'éviter toute future déconvenue.
- Faire un script qui permette de lister les années non testées pour une prestation spécifique afin de pouvoir efficacement enrichir les tests pour les années non testées.

issue_title: Support defining process requirements like Docker image
issue_body:
Support process syntax for defining requirements. We should be able to define the Docker image. Review how this is supported in CWL.

When reviewing CWL, also research how they specify resource requirements like cores, RAM... If not too hard, support defining that too.

You will have to change JSON schemas we use for syntax validation, register function and the ORM model. 

The first part of this task is to support that bioinformaticians will be able to define this requirements. The second part is to support loading specific Docker images before process execution. We will ignore the resource requirements until we support Slurm.

I'm an expert node.js developer, have worked with serverless framework and firebase functions
I recently started working with Chalice framework, it is nice and easy to get start but something is wrong with dependencies, I'm still unable to deploy it with dependencies

it all starts with when I made a hello world program and run locally with `chalice local` command and then deploy on aws with `chalice deploy` it worked quite fine
then i tried to include pyrebase(https://github.com/thisbejim/Pyrebase#database) a python library for firebase

code is here for reference:
```

def myFunction():
    # This is the JSON body which is sent in POST request.

    if jsonBody.get('result').get('action') == 'wellcome':

        return {"speech": "Wellcome to the bot, say book hotel to book it any time"}

issue_title: Logging from manticore module doesn't appear
issue_body: The 'MANTICORE' logger seems to have its default level set to logging.WARNING. In `manticore.verbosity` we never adjust the logger level for the manticore module itself, so all its logging never actually appears.

issue_title: Different Values on CPU vs GPU.
issue_body:
[I was creating a NER tagger on Conll 2003 data based on the Ma and Hovy tagger.](https://github.com/blester125/dynet/blob/competative-tagger/examples/tagger/conv_blstm_crf.py)

I was getting really poor performance.
```
[tagger] Best f1 was 0.908426 at epoch 14
[tagger] Stopping at epoch 55 due to failure to improve.
[tagger] Test F1: 0.853864
```
This poor performance was both at the end of training and after the first epoch I was getting dev F1 around `78` compared to the F1 of around `85` I normally see in a similar tagger I have written in pytorch.

I then ran the tagger on the gpu and suddenly I getting much better performance.
```
[tagger] Best f1 was 0.934888 at epoch 36
[tagger] Stopping at epoch 77 due to failure to improve.
[tagger] Test F1: 0.890261
```
```
[tagger] Best f1 was 0.935584 at epoch 15
[tagger] Stopping at epoch 56 due to failure to improve.
[tagger] Test F1: 0.895778
```
I was also seeing F1 scores after the first epoch that were comparable to the scores from the the pytorch tagger.

I investigated this a bit more and it seems that running things on CPU vs GPU produce very different results. I created a [script here](https://github.com/blester125/dynet/blob/competative-tagger/examples/tagger/dynet_cpu_gpu.py) a simple convolution and tries to optimize the result to zero. 

Running it you can see that the final values of parameters are very different:

 * Parts of the embeddings layer
``` 
CPU:
[ 0.09190765 -0.35686716 -0.08511744  0.62517864  0.27539271] 
GPU:
[ 0.0408158  -0.3782683  -0.09938584  0.61804533  0.38606855]
```
 * Parts of the Conv weights
```
CPU:
[[[[-0.00934861 -0.45052513  0.38271371  0.37630072 -0.23521839
     0.05024116  0.03176426  0.07979026  0.42238858 -0.27316907]
GPU:
[[[[ 0.0184729  -0.422703    0.41053453  0.40412155 -0.20739615
     0.0780626   0.05958565  0.10761151  0.45020884 -0.24534746]
```
 * Parts of the Conv Bias
```
CPU:
[-0.34024909  0.05928647 -0.36520264 -0.31748942  0.42725024  0.25176921
  0.38832936  0.32116064 -0.14631058 -0.34010348] 
GPU:
[-0.33341098  0.06612484 -0.35836464 -0.31065124  0.43408799  0.25860667
  0.39516699  0.32799882 -0.13947366 -0.33326536]
```

Not only are the final weights different but the final result is very different with the GPU getting much closer to the goal than the CPU version:
```
CPU Loss: [0.85731989]
GPU Loss: [0.28159717]
```

I am using the `MomentumSGDTrainer` and I thought at first it might have something to do with the sparse updates because it mentioned they are not strictly correct for Momentum but even turning them off still has this error. When I run this script with just the `SimpleSGDTrainer` this discrepancy between GPU and CPU goes away.

issue_title: Memory used by the computation graph
issue_body: Hi. So my program (in C++) runs out of memory on the GPU after a few hours of runtime. I want to know if there is any way to find out how much memory is being used by the computation graph, for instance, for a few sentences/minibatch in my training data?

issue_title: [Intents] Handling question marks
issue_body:
Hi guys !

I'm running into a small problem today, with the question mark '**?**' character.
I noticed that when I fit my model's intent with a sentence composed of a question mark (at the end of the sentence), when I input the sentence with and without the question mark, I get different results and important probability score diff.

When trained **with** question mark :
```
{ input: 'es-tu réel',
  slots: [],
  intent: { intentName: 'whoAreYouFR', probability: 0.46640502699361525 } }
```
```
{ input: 'es-tu réel ?',
  slots: [],
  intent: { intentName: 'whoAreYouFR', probability: 1 } }
```

When trained **without** question mark :
```
{ input: 'es-tu réel',
  slots: [],
  intent: { intentName: 'whoAreYouFR', probability: 1 } }
```
```
{ input: 'es-tu réél ?',
  slots: [],
  intent: { intentName: 'whoAreYouFR', probability: 0.40673316662106906 } }
```

When trained **with both** sentences 'es-tu réel' and 'es-tu réel ?' :
```
{ input: 'es-tu réel ?',
  slots: [],
  intent: { intentName: 'whoAreYouFR', probability: 1 } }
```
```
{ input: 'es-tu réel',
  slots: [],
  intent: { intentName: 'whoAreYouFR', probability: 1 } }
```

Do you have any idea why it's doing this ? Is it normal ?

Thanks in advance ! :)

issue_title: What should be done about non-atomicity of Table.counter_set()
issue_body:
See #1541 for discussion.

There is a [note][1] from the HappyBase source code

> Note that application code should *never* store a incremented or decremented counter value directly; use the atomic `Table.counter_inc` and `Table.counter_dec` methods for that.

and we just went one step further and just made `Table.counter_set()` a not implemented method (it raises `NotImplementedError` when called).

[1]: https://github.com/wbolster/happybase/blob/9cbd718c10a3089f234f1eac1236b631e1f8e7cd/happybase/table.py#L531-L534

issue_title: Python Logging API always defaults to Custom logs
issue_body:
When using the **Python Stackdriver Logging API**, there is not a well-defined way to specify where you want your logs to land. All of them will go into Custom Logs. The way around this is to use a Logging Handler, but this is not well written anywhere except for this one particular blog post:

https://medium.com/google-cloud/cloud-logging-though-a-python-log-handler-a3fbeaf14704#.39a97qpib

The reason it cannot be done through the standard API is because this line 

https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/logging/google/cloud/logging/logger.py#L127

overwrites whatever resource you would rather write to. No parameter I could find (or the Google engineer I asked) would set the resource in order to change the destination of the logs. The closest we could find is here:

https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/logging/google/cloud/logging/connection.py#L131

but this is set by the previous line stated above. Changing this resource manually is a pretty important feature to us that we are surprised is not already there.

issue_title: firestore.CreateIfMissingOption should be replaced
issue_body:
Paraphrased from @samtstern:

It's still possible to do

```python
    city_ref.update({
        u'capital': True
    }, firestore.CreateIfMissingOption(True))
```

The other Firestore SDKs have removed this option and moved to an option on the `set()` operation called, `merge()`.  For example, in the Android SDK:

```java
db.collection("cities").document("BJ")
        .set(data, SetOptions.merge());
```

The new semantics make it so that the `set()` operation (and `create()` which some SDKs offer) is the only operation which can make a new document.  The `merge()` option means that the data will be deep merged into any existing document, rather than just obliterating and replacing.

4. Stacktrace if available
5. Steps to reproduce
* run my python pubsub-consumer (in uwsgi with 1 thread) for a bit more than one hour
* no special log messages about the application restarting
* observe high cpu usage
 
6. Code example
summary of relevant parts:
```
        subscriber = pubsub.SubscriberClient()
        project_path = subscriber.project_path(project_name)
        subscription_path = subscriber.subscription_path(project_name, subscription_name)
        topic_path = subscriber.topic_path(project_name, topic_name)

        self.pubsub_consumer_sub = subscriber.subscribe(subscription_path)
        self.pubsub_consumer_sub.on_exception = self._error_cb

        self.pubsub_consumer_sub.open(self._message_callback)
```
where _message_callback tries to do message.ack() if all was ok in application code and otherwise raises.

Relevant debugging:
```

   43 root      20   0 1580028  80752  15568 S  0.0  0.3   0:01.13 uwsgi
```
then take the process with the high cpu and strace it:

```
strace -e trace=all -p 711

....
poll([{fd=15, events=POLLIN}, {fd=17, events=0}, {fd=20, events=0}, {fd=21, events=POLLIN}], 4, 154) = 2 ([{fd=17, revents=POLLHUP}, {fd=20, revents=POLLHUP}])

poll([{fd=15, events=POLLIN}, {fd=17, events=0}, {fd=20, events=0}, {fd=21, events=POLLIN}], 4, 154) = 2 ([{fd=17, revents=POLLHUP}, {fd=20, revents=POLLHUP}])

poll([{fd=15, events=POLLIN}, {fd=17, events=0}, {fd=20, events=0}, {fd=21, events=POLLIN}], 4, 154) = 2 ([{fd=17, revents=POLLHUP}, {fd=20, revents=POLLHUP}])
clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC, {693581, 541081120}) = 0
....(and many more)

Taking a look at the open sockets, something jumps out:
```
ss -t -a
State      Recv-Q Send-Q                                                          Local Address:Port                                                              Peer Address:Port 
CLOSE-WAIT 1      0                                                                 10.0.20.188:59188                                                            74.125.206.84:https
CLOSE-WAIT 1      0                                                                 10.0.20.188:38434                                                            74.125.206.84:https
```

Note that these target IP addresses (74.125.206.84) are probably PubSub related, but I cannot really confirm that. Debugging over https obviously is a bit difficult as well.

The loop of clock_gettime + poll (with POLLHUP) in combination with the two sockets in CLOSE-WAIT with a Recv-Q of "1" leads me to believe there is somehow a bug in the Google PubSub libraries where an invalid/incomplete message is hanging in the receive queue, the library keeps trying to consume the message and then retries etc etc, causing a hot loop taking quite a lot of cpu.

issue_title: firestore: add a FieldPath class
issue_body:
The firestore client conflates dot-separated strings with field paths in the `update` methods. There should be two cases for top-level dict keys passed to `update`:

- If the key is a string, it is split on dots and cannot contain any of the special characters `~*/[]`. There also can't be any empty parts. One reasonable approach is:
    ```
    # validate that there are no special characters in s
    fp = FieldPath(s.split('.'))
    ```
- If the key is a `FieldPath`, it is treated as-is.

Where `FieldPath` is something like:
```
class FieldPath(object):
    def __init__(self, *parts):
        # Verify that each part is a string, and that no part is the empty string.

    def _to_api_repr(self):
        # Apply Firestore escaping rules, using backticks and backslashes for parts
        # that do not match ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z_0-9]*$.
        # E.g. FieldPath("a", "~", "`").to_api_repr() => "a.`~`.`\``"

    def __eq__(self): # and __ne__ and whatever else is needed to be a dict key.
```
Since it is now possible to pass a dict where the same effective path occurs twice, that needs to be checked explicitly.
E.g.
```
{"a": 1, FieldPath("a"): 2}
```
should be an error.

@schmidt-sebastian Confirm this is accurate.

issue_title: [1.1.0-pre0] jar tool skipping if target includes META-INF/MANIFEST.MF
issue_body:
We have a use case for including an additional `META-INF/MANIFEST.MF` in the bundle.
`BUILD` file:
```
resources(name='tool',
  sources=globs('META-INF/MANIFEST.MF')
)
```
With e1b2d6808f8fe2cdc6964a7f1efd816e2678c70e  switching to `jar-tool`, original `MANIFEST.MF` is skipped, now we get:
```
Manifest-Version: 1.0
Created-By: org.pantsbuild.tools.jar.JarBuilder
```
instead of
```
Manifest-Version: 1.0
Main-Class: com.twitter.scalding.Tool
```

cc @jsirois @mateor

issue_title: Pants cannot find tools.jar with JDK9
issue_body: [JEP 220: Modular Run-Time Images](http://openjdk.java.net/jeps/220) implemented in JDK9 removed tools.jar from the jdk image. As a result I get a filure when running pants: `FAILURE: Failed to locate tools.jar library`. Since Jigsaw has the potential to be a major headache, it would be nice it we could starting running test targets early. For that to happen JDK9 issues with Pants must be addressed.

issue_title: Make --enable-v2-engine the default
issue_body:
After #3740 and https://groups.google.com/d/msg/pants-devel/odF_abSXuvU/X3UqqvqfBwAJ and fixing the primary memory issue with the new engine, we're nearly ready to enable it by default (possibly still blocked by #4172).

Doing this would involve flipping the default for the `--enable-v2-engine` flag, and then fixing any test fallout from that change.

issue_title: `--no-compile-zinc-incremental` does not purge existing classfiles
issue_body: `--no-incremental` causes target analysis to be empty each time. But it does not also purge the output classes directory, so it means that non-incremental compiles can still suffer from issues like https://github.com/sbt/zinc/issues/144 (which seem to be caused by the classfiles already being present on disk).

issue_title: Rationalize Target and Task test infra
issue_body:
Today there are several test baseclasses to pick from and some overlapping support functionality provided by each.  Rationalize test infra to provide:
+ a regular way to contribute support for constructing new targets programatically - both synthetic and concrete
+ a clear way execute a Task as a unit including product preparation and engine lifecycle so the use can focus on output product testing (console or fs based products)

A follow on will be documenting this at a high level in https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/issues/447

issue_title: Improve performance related to content addressability
issue_body:
@stuhood  did profiling for `./pants run src/python/pants/engine/exp/legacy:dependencies -- ::`, looks like we spend significant amount of time pickling.

There are some low hanging fruits like 

* computing a key just once for cache get/put so we don't pickle it twice to look it up in the cache, and then again to put it in the cache
* adding a small inline cache of un-pickled objects by-Key (to avoid having to unpickle them if they are already unpickled)
* Also lmdb high miss rate (`step_id` should not be included in the fingerprint) may also contributed.

While that helps `get`, we probably still need to be smart about what to content address for `put`.

We know in minimum we need to cache `task_node`, now we cache everything, i.e, 100% in keys. We will need a representation that's compatible with both keys and content.

issue_body: My project is scala code with Pants 1.0.0, and specs2 for unit tests. When running `./pants test ::` I get output that looks like: https://gist.github.com/nitay/4be43ab9b0aaf8bbdfa7a1f453d5cd2c. Seems like there should be some better numbers there on the totals and time lines?

issue_title: DuplicateDetector: broken for jvm_binary that has `source=...`,  does not work for rest.
issue_body:
@viktortnk reported the following backtrace upgrading from 0.0.51 to 0.0.52 or 0.0.53:
```
File "/root/.cache/pants/setup/bootstrap/pants.zgaZaX/install/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pants/backend/jvm/tasks/detect_duplicates.py", line 119, in _get_conflicts_by_artifacts
    conflicts_by_artifacts[tuple(sorted(artifacts))].add(file_name)
  File "/root/.cache/pants/setup/bootstrap/pants.zgaZaX/install/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pants/build_graph/target.py", line 510, in __lt__
    return self.address < other.address

Exception message: 'unicode' object has no attribute ‘address’ 
```

The `sorted(artifacts)` are a mixture of jar paths for external dependencies and Targets for internal dependencies, the mixture is built [here](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/tasks/detect_duplicates.py#L60-L70).  This mixture breaks the Target `__lt__` operator method - its not robust to the mix.  So this is the hard break.  It _should_ be a hard break for ~every JvmBinary target - to have a binary you need a source (internal dep of self), or 1 or more dependencies on internal jvm targets to provide the main the binary runs.  This leads to the soft break - the internal class and resource detection stops at the binary, but should be recursive.  That bug is [here](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/tasks/detect_duplicates.py#L91).

issue_title: Kolibri should explicitly set cookie name
issue_body:
<!--
Instructions:
 * Fill out the sections below, replace …'s with information about your issue
 * Use the 'preview' function above this text box to verify formatting before submitting
-->

### Observed behavior

When running Kolibri and Studio at the same time in the same browser, Kolibri will immediately sign the user out and require them to sign in again. This process will repeat for as long as both apps are running.

This happens because Kolibri and Studio both currently use the Django default cookie name, 'sessionid'. This causes Kolibri and Studio to set each other's sessionid after various actions.

### Expected behavior
When you sign in, you stay signed in, Studio or not. :)

### User-facing consequences
This is primarily an internal developer issue, though it could happen on devices that deploy other local services in addition to Kolibri that are using Django and keep the default cookie name.

### Steps to reproduce
- Start Studio and sign in
- Start Kolibri and sign in
- Return to Kolibri and try to perform an action, like importing content
Result: It says you are signed out and asks you to sign in again. Once you sign in again, this process will repeat.

### Context
- Latest Kolibri from develop branch as of 6-28-18
- Latest Studio from develop branch as of 6-28-18
- OS X 10.13 (High Sierra)
- Any browser, tested with Safari, Chrome, Chromium, and FIrefox

issue_title: Rules with olderThan and newerThan comparisons are failing
issue_body:
When I run Scout2 (Mac OSX, installed with pip) I'm getting some rule failures:

Failed to process rule defined in iam-root-account-used-recently.json
'<' not supported between instances of 'int' and 'str'
Failed to process rule defined in iam-user-no-Active-key-rotation.json
'>' not supported between instances of 'int' and 'str'
Failed to process rule defined in iam-user-no-Inactive-key-rotation.json
'>' not supported between instances of 'int' and 'str'

The failing rules all appear to be related to date comparisons.

Looking at the Opinel codebase, it looks like the expected argument to the 'olderThan' and 'newerThan' operators should be an array of two elements - an integer and a units as a string.  For example, [90, "days"].

But in these JSON files, the argument being passed into the operators is a [string, string].  In one case the array is ["90", "days"].  And in the other the argument is ["_ARG_1_", "days"].  I'm not sure how to format the JSON in the latter case so the value is converted to an integer, or I would submit a PR.

issue_title: Extraneous characters display in replay possibly introduced by wget
issue_body:
I have encountered an issue where extraneous characters are being displayed for simple WARCs in replay. For example, https://github.com/oduwsdl/ipwb/blob/master/ipwb/samples/warcs/froggie.warc.gz shows extraneous 'ac' characters before and '0' after the content. This content is not on the live web capture at http://www.cs.odu.edu/~mkelly/semester/2017_spring/remoteFroggie.html .

The salam WARCs in the core sample data set also have extraneous characters.

Upon further inspection of the froggie warc, this content is inside the WARC, so it's not introduced by ipwb. This may be due to wget's WARC implementation introducing the additional characters, the latter produces with wget 1.18 on macOS.

For froggie I used the command:
`wget -pH --warc-file=froggie http://www.cs.odu.edu/~mkelly/semester/2017_spring/remoteFroggie.html`

issue_title: The GOR4 C code occasionally causes a SEGV
issue_body:
@bamueh discovered this case:

```python
from light.database import DatabaseSpecifier
from light.backend import Backend
from dark.reads import SSAARead

db = DatabaseSpecifier().getDatabaseFromKeywords(
    landmarks=['GOR4AlphaHelix'],
    trigPoints=[])

backend = Backend()
backend.configure(db.dbParams)

list(backend.scan(SSAARead('1TMT:I', 'XPR', '-B-')))
```

```sh
$ python /tmp/bug.py
fish: Job 2, 'python /tmp/bug.py ' terminated by signal SIGSEGV (Address boundary error)
```

The error occurs on line 809 of `src/gor4/gor4-base.c` (the 2nd line below):

```c
	for(ires1 = Lim1; ires1 <= Lim2; ires1++)
	  pred[ires1] = conf[KeepNterm];
```

and as far as we can tell only happens on a read with a very short length (3).

issue_title: Support for DHCP on MGT interface

There is no way to configure MGT interface with DHCP. `device.SystemSettings` object doesn't support that type of configuration. 
It could be set via CLI with the command `set deviceconfig system type dhcp-client` or configuring properly the path `/config/devices/entry[@name='localhost.localdomain']/deviceconfig/system/type/dhcp-client` in XML device config.

issue_title: All values in lxc_conf are passed to docker-egine as string
issue_body:
Hi,

I know that LXC will be deprecated, but I've noticed a small bug: all values in lxc_conf passed in host_config when creating containers are converted to string. This causes some issues with options like 'lxc.cgroup.cpu.shares' as docker-engine expects an int when unmarshalling the json, so any value expected as non-string from the docker-engine side will fail:

```
ERRO[173466] Handler for POST /v1.21/containers/create returned error: json: cannot unmarshal string into Go value of type int64 
ERRO[173466] HTTP Error                                    err=json: cannot unmarshal string into Go value of type int64 statusCode=500
```

The issue is in utils/utils.py, L621:
```
    if isinstance(lxc_conf, dict):
        formatted = []
        for k, v in six.iteritems(lxc_conf):
            formatted.append({'Key': k, 'Value': str(v)})
        lxc_conf = formatted
```

A quick and dirty solution would be to replace `str(v)` for `v` so the decision on the type used would recall on the user. 

Otherwise, we could go farther and map the types expected from docker-engine for every `lxc-conf` value in order to match the docker API.

Tested on this gear:
```
$ pip freeze | grep docker-py && python --version && docker version && uname -a && lsb_release -a
docker-py==1.6.0
Python 2.7.6
Client:
 Version:      1.9.1

Linux dockertest01 3.19.0-39-generic #44~14.04.1-Ubuntu SMP Wed Dec 2 10:00:35 UTC 2015 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
No LSB modules are available.

I could try to provide a pull request if necessary.

issue_title: Add support for Docker v2 registries
issue_body:
I'm here via Saltstack, and troubleshooting a issue with connectivity while doing a pull. The problem is that the library is hardcoded to reference the v1 API to verify SSL connectivity to a local registry, which does not work with v2 registry.

The request should be changed to also try and ping `https:// + hostname + "/v2/"` in addition to `'https://' + hostname + '/v1/_ping'`

The rest of the pull stream works OK, since it is connecting to Docker, not the repo directly otherwise.

See: https://github.com/docker/docker-py/blob/master/docker/auth/auth.py#L32

issue_title: mti sources are not handled correctly
issue_body:
I spent the afternoon debugging why I'm not getting any GSUB/GPOS/GDEF out of the pipeline, turned out to be because of https://github.com/googlei18n/fontmake/commit/9c2ae2d6ee984b82ab2db741fdccf800e20b4061

while fixing that, I also made a change to how mti sources are consumed: https://github.com/googlei18n/fontmake/commit/1e2a639788a8d06564d1fe46a43d2e1bed03bb14

that exposed another bug.  Copying from the commit message there:
```
Make mti-sources processing more strict
If --mti-sources is provided, then it MUST declare mti sources for
all masters, so don't silently fallback to automatic feature
generation.

In fact, this exposed a bug in fontmake.  When running against
NotoSansDevanagari sources, we now get:

>> Saving OTF for NotoSansDevanagari-Light
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/bin/fontmake", line 9, in <module>
    load_entry_point('fontmake==1.0', 'console_scripts', 'fontmake')()
  File "build/bdist.linux-x86_64/egg/fontmake/__main__.py", line 42, in main
  File "build/bdist.linux-x86_64/egg/fontmake/font_project.py", line 221, in run_from_glyphs
  File "build/bdist.linux-x86_64/egg/fontmake/font_project.py", line 258, in run_from_ufos
  File "build/bdist.linux-x86_64/egg/fontmake/font_project.py", line 113, in build_otfs
  File "build/bdist.linux-x86_64/egg/fontmake/font_project.py", line 157, in save_otfs
KeyError: 'NotoSansDevanagari-Light'

That is because the sources call this font Thin, and the plist has that
and the mti-sources are called that.  But fontmake ignored the name
of the master and replaced it with Light, and is looking for Light.

That should be fixed separately.
```

issue_title: Inconsistent behavior between source 0.4.1 and PyPI 0.4.1
issue_body:
I believe there is an issue with the version of pims (nominally 0.4.1) available through PyPI and installed via `pip install pims`. Specifically, the behavior relates to the `as_grey` parameter in the `ImageSequence` class, which was removed in [this pims commit](https://github.com/soft-matter/pims/commit/f97634337afccc8be01d10f35c4165d2bbca011b#diff-00de0855a42a33014c23a4ba27e397d9) back in January 2017.

The [pims in PyPI](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/PIMS) lists the latest release as 0.4.1, which was [released in September](https://github.com/soft-matter/pims/releases). (This is the pims I was using to regenerate the notebooks.) This version *should* have had the `as_grey` parameter removed, since the commit that removed it was made back in January, but the pims installed by PyPI does not remove the `as_grey` parameter. Building pims direct from source does remove the `as_grey` parameter and raises an exception if the parameter is used.

I made [a gist](https://gist.github.com/charlesreid1/f37b5c5761fa1a5c89cbde17ffcdeaef) that illustrates what's going on - it includes commands to set up a Docker container, build pims, and load a simple pims ImageSequence using the `as_grey` parameter under two separate scenarios: using pims from source, and using pims from PyPI. This uses the standard [travis-garnet](https://hub.docker.com/r/travisci/ci-garnet/tags/) image from Dockerhub, and runs some commands taken almost verbatim from the .travis.yml file in the pims repo. You can see that installing pims from source leads to an error when using the `as_grey` parameter, but installing pims via pip does not. 

I believe what's happening is an older, pre-January 2017 version of pims was bundled and submitted to PyPI under the moniker of 0.4.1. (The version string is 0.4.1 in both cases, although the behavior is definitely different.)

issue_title: Fix a10 driver to automatically fix incorrect delay/timeout values
issue_body: Timeout cannot be greater than Delay for health monitors.  Delay is the wait time between health monitor checks and Timeout is the amount of time we wait until we've decided something is "unhealthy".  If timeout is larger than delay, a second health check will be sent before the first health check has a chance to timeout.

issue_title: Clarification: how scalable is deis-registry?
issue_body:
`deisctl` permits the `deis-registry` component to be "scaled," and comments in the `deisctl` code specifically call it out as being one of the few components that can currently scale.

But when I look at unit files for deployed applications, they all get the registry address from the etcd keey `/deis/registry/host`.

So, I'm looking for some small bit of clarification here.  Am I missing something?  Or does "scalable" here actually have more to do with creating warm standbys than it does with scaling capacity?  If additional registries are nothing but warm standbys, what advantage (if any) does creating those offer over, say, simply preseeding all nodes with the Deis component images?

If nothing else, there's an opportunity to address this in the documentation, which I am happy to work on once I understand this better.

I want to drain all logs to ElasticSearch. I'm using AWS ElasticSearch service that uses HTTP protocol and doesn't support TCP (http://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticsearch-service/latest/developerguide/aes-limits.html).

As I see in Deis documentation the logger component only supports TCP and UDP connections. 

Is there anyway to connect the logger component with AWS ElasticSearch service?

issue_title: logout: --silent
issue_body: Add a --silent option to logout, to suppress all of informational output.   Useful for scripting.

issue_title: Allow the Transfer service to figure out what the default path should be when `ls`ing
issue_body:
Instead of having the `globus transfer ls` command default to `/~/`, don't send any `path` to the Transfer service and let the Transfer service figure out what the default path is supposed to be.

Fixes S3 endpoints (which don't support `/~/`), shared endpoints (which should usually default to `/`), and any kind of endpoint that has had a `default_directory` configured.

issue_title: Unhandled exception crashes Python process
issue_body:
When connections are lost, the Python process terminates with the following error message:
```
terminate called after throwing an instance of 'cpp_odbc::error'
  what():  ODBC error
state: 40004
native error code: -1
message: [EXASOL][EXASolution driver]Connection lost.
Aborted
```
It seems that the `cpp_odbc::error` is not translated to a `turbodbc::error` that would be known to the Python interpreter.

issue_title: LazyList.__getitem__ assumes i is a number but it can be a slice
issue_body:
LazyList's __getitem__ implementation [assumes **i** is always a number](https://github.com/ethereum/pyrlp/blob/develop/rlp/lazy.py#L108), but it can [be a slice as well](https://docs.python.org/2/reference/datamodel.html#object.__getitem__)

The fix is trivial, but I want to investigate it further because it's not clear to me how that deserialize() call would cause __getitem__ to raise an IndexError, since it [checks the list's length before accessing the first element](https://github.com/ethereum/pyrlp/blob/develop/rlp/sedes/big_endian_int.py#L38)

Here's an example traceback I got from running the hive consensus simulator against pyethapp

TypeErrorTraceback (most recent call last)
<ipython-input-1-59579d93b561> in <module>()
----> 1 from importblock import Importer; Importer(eth).run()

/importblock.py in run(self)
     22             data = open('/blocks/' + block, 'r').read()
     23             block_data = rlp.decode_lazy(data)
---> 24             header = BlockHeader.deserialize(block_data[0])
     25             transactions = rlp.sedes.CountableList(Transaction).deserialize(block_data[1])
     26             uncles = rlp.sedes.CountableList(BlockHeader).deserialize(block_data[2])

/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/rlp-0.5.1-py2.7.egg/rlp/sedes/lists.pyc in deserialize(cls, serial, exclude, mutable, **kwargs)
    243     def deserialize(cls, serial, exclude=None, mutable=False, **kwargs):
    244         try:
--> 245             values = cls.get_sedes().deserialize(serial)
    246         except ListDeserializationError as e:
    247             raise ObjectDeserializationError(serial=serial, sedes=cls, list_exception=e)

/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/rlp-0.5.1-py2.7.egg/rlp/sedes/lists.pyc in deserialize(self, serial)
     81             if not (sedes_consumed or elements_consumed):
     82                 try:
---> 83                     result.append(sedes.deserialize(element))
     84                 except DeserializationError as e:
     85                     raise ListDeserializationError(serial=serial, element_exception=e, index=index)

/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/rlp-0.5.1-py2.7.egg/rlp/sedes/big_endian_int.pyc in deserialize(self, serial)
     36             raise DeserializationError('Invalid serialization (wrong size)',

---> 38         if self.l is None and len(serial) > 0 and serial[0:1] == ascii_chr(0):

issue_title: Refine logic for Trinity attach command.
issue_body:
link: https://github.com/ethereum/py-evm/issues/712
### What is wrong?

Need to make `$ trinity attach` behave in the following way.

### How can it be fixed

When `$ trinity attach` is run with no other arguments, the following logic should be done.

- looks for `jsonrpc.ipc` *all* chain folders found in the `$TRINITY_HOME`.  
  - If exactly 1 is found, displays what IPC socket it's connecting to and launches console.
  - if 0 or `>1` are found, displays appropriate error.

However, the path the socket should be able to be specified as a positional argument to the command as `$ trinity attach /path/to/jsonrpc.ipc`.  When the path is specified this way trinity should log the path to the IPC socket it is connecting to and launch the console.

issue_title: Make command-line scripts "entry points"
issue_body:
The command-line scripts `pyDelphin` and `mrs.py` would be better distributed as an "entry point" defined in setup.py.

See here: http://pythonhosted.org/setuptools/setuptools.html#automatic-script-creation

See how the Xigt project does it:
- https://github.com/xigt/xigt/blob/master/setup.py#L90
- https://github.com/xigt/xigt/blob/master/xigt/main.py

issue_title: conda install widgetsnbextension doesn't copy files to share folder
issue_body:
After doing:

    conda skeleton pypi widgetsnbextension
    conda build widgetsnbextension

Conda succeeds producing `.tar.bz2` file.

How after installing it with:

    conda install widgetsnbextension
    
Trying to activate it with:

    jupyter nbextension enable --py widgetsnbextension
    
Fails with error:

    Enabling notebook extension jupyter-js-widgets/extension...
          - Validating: problems found:
            - require?  X jupyter-js-widgets/extension
            
The reason for this is that conda did not copy `jupyter` folder from the egg file to `share` folder of environment directory.
After manually copying the above activation works fine.


Content of `widgetsnbextension-1.2.3-py2.7.egg` in `.tar.bz2` file produced by `conda`:

    .\EGG-INFO\
        .\...
    .\share\
        .\jupyter\
            .\...
    .\widgetsnbextension\
        .\...
    
Expected contend of `Anaconda\env\env_name` folder:
    
    .\...
    .\share
        .\jupyter\
            .\...
    .\...
    

My question is:

Should `conda` be able to copy those files automatically like `pip` does?

Or is there something else that should be done with recipes files to ensure these files are copied?

Thanks

issue_title: Adding a non-existent package as an optional constraint causes conda to prefer previous versions without the constraint
issue_body:
**I'm submitting a...** - [x] bug report - [ ] feature request ## Current Behavior I have a noarch package, https://anaconda.org/ceball/test, which in version 0.3.0 has no `run_constrained` in its recipe (i.e. no `constrains` in the built package). In version 0.3.1, I added one package (`poppler <=0.60.1`) to `run_constrained`. If I run `conda install -c ceball/label/dev test` on linux or mac, test 0.3.1 is installed as expected. However, if I run that command on windows, test 0.3.0 is installed. Note that poppler does not exist for windows. I can install 0.3.1 on windows either by running `conda install -c ceball/label/dev "test>=0.3.1" or by removing test 0.3.0 from ceball/label/dev and running `conda install -c ceball/label/dev`. ### Steps to Reproduce On linux/mac, the following gets you test 0.3.1, but on windows, you get test 0.3.0: ``` $ conda install -c ceball/label/dev ``` The diff between 0.3.0 and 0.3.1: ``` index 4f2f069..e1bff57 100644 --- a/conda.recipe/meta.yaml +++ b/conda.recipe/meta.yaml @@ -1,6 +1,6 @@ package: name: test - version: 0.3.0 + version: 0.3.1 source: path: .. @@ -16,9 +16,9 @@ requirements: - setuptools run: - fiona -# run_constrained: -# # indirect dep of fiona; pinned to fix https://travis-ci.org/pyviz/pyviz/jobs/383650789#L2372 -# - poppler <=0.60.1 + run_constrained: + # indirect dep of fiona; pinned to fix https://travis-ci.org/pyviz/pyviz/jobs/383650789#L2372 + - poppler <=0.60.1 test: imports: ``` The packages are available at https://anaconda.org/ceball/test/files. The source is attached as [test.tar.gz](https://github.com/conda/conda/files/2044573/test.tar.gz). ## Expected Behavior I was expecting test 0.3.1 to be installed on windows as well as mac and linux when I run `conda install -c ceball/label/dev test`. I.e. I expected run_constrained/constraints not to affect platforms where a constrained package is unavailable. Sorry if I am just confused about the purpose of/how to use optional constraints! ## Environment Information
`conda info`

issue_title: Replace pycrypto with pycryptodome in conda and conda-build
issue_body: Conda and conda-build are using `pycrypto` but this packages has some unfixed security issues and has not been updated in a while. [pycryptodome is a drop-in-replacement](https://github.com/Legrandin/pycryptodome). Maybe Conda can switch to this package?

issue_title: conda spending much time looking through files in package cache (conda 4.3.13)
issue_body:
Hi - 
this is kind of hard to look for in the issues so I apologize in advance if this has already been brought up.  Recently, any conda operation has been extremely slow. For example, a simple conda search takes 5 minutes:

```
$ time conda search pysam
[...snip...]
real    4m58.644s
user    3m3.074s
sys     0m46.633s
```
This was with 3 channels active. Nearly the same time with just the defaults channel. As a quick and dirty way to try and figure out what is happening I did an strace with time stamps
```
strace -fro conda_search_strace conda search pysam
```
That ran for 272s
```
$ awk '{t+=$2}END{print t}' conda_search_strace
272.496
```

Most of that time (2/3) is spent stat-ing files in the package cache:
```
$ grep 'stat' conda_search_strace | grep '/usr/local/Anaconda/pkgs' | awk '{t+=$2}END{print t}'
178.849
```

Our package cache is large (68GiB - it's a central install used by admins and users to create many environments so it's hard to clean up) and located on a more-or-less busy NFS mounted file system.  Indeed at less busy times this operation runs faster, though still 2 minutes, which is still somewhat painful.

So my questions are (1) did something change in conda that would explain this slowdown? (2) is there anything we can do? Or conversely - are we doing something to cause this? (3) could whatever conda does that requires touching everything in the package cache be optimized? caching? database?

Anyway - please forgive me if i missed something obvious.

issue_title: "conda search" produces junk output
issue_body:
It seems there's no way to turn off the "Fetching package metadata ....." message from conda search.  This makes it hard to use for tests, like this:

```
has_matplotlib=`conda search matplotlib --names-only`
```

And it's essentially useless output.  At the very least, a "-q" switch to suppress it would be useful.

While we're at it, "conda search" could exit with non-zero status to indicate no packages found.

issue_title: Newest compiler_compat changes seem to break python setup.py scripts
issue_body:
Here is an attempt to build a trivial C extension via Python setup.py on an essentially new miniconda install, with 'defaults' gcc installed:

```
$ conda install gcc
Fetching package metadata ...........
Solving package specifications: .

The following NEW packages will be INSTALLED:

    cloog: 0.18.0-0        
    gcc:   4.8.5-7         
    gmp:   6.1.2-hb3b607b_0
    isl:   0.12.2-0        
    mpc:   1.0.3-hf803216_4
    mpfr:  3.1.5-h12ff648_1
$ python setup.py build
running build
running build_ext
building 'foo' extension
creating build
creating build/temp.linux-x86_64-3.6
gcc -pthread -B /private/home/ezyang/pytorch/env/compiler_compat -Wsign-compare -DNDEBUG -g -fwrapv -O3 -Wall -Wstrict-prototypes -fPIC -I/private/home/ezyang/pytorch/env/include/python3.6m -c foo.c -o build/temp.linux-x86_64-3.6/foo.o
creating build/lib.linux-x86_64-3.6
gcc -pthread -shared -B /private/home/ezyang/pytorch/env/compiler_compat -L/private/home/ezyang/pytorch/env/lib -Wl,-rpath=/private/home/ezyang/pytorch/env/lib -Wl,--no-as-needed -Wl,--sysroot=/ build/temp.linux-x86_64-3.6/foo.o -L/private/home/ezyang/pytorch/env/lib -lpython3.6m -o build/lib.linux-x86_64-3.6/foo.cpython-36m-x86_64-linux-gnu.so
/private/home/ezyang/pytorch/env/compiler_compat/ld: cannot find -lpthread
/private/home/ezyang/pytorch/env/compiler_compat/ld: cannot find -lc
collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status
error: command 'gcc' failed with exit status 1
$ cat setup.py
from distutils.core import setup, Extension
setup(name='foo',
      version='1.0',
      ext_modules=[Extension('foo', ['foo.c'])],
      )
$ cat foo.c
$ 
```

You can see that there is some business with `-B /private/home/ezyang/pytorch/env/compiler_compat`; this particular line is breaking the build when used with 'defaults' gcc. Using my system gcc there is no problem (i.e., omit the `conda install gcc`)

Downloading source to cache: jpegsrc.v8d.tar.gz
Downloading http://www.ijg.org/files/jpegsrc.v8d.tar.gz
Error: HTTPError: 403 Client Error: Forbidden for url: http://www.ijg.org/files/jpegsrc.v8d.tar.gz: http://www.ijg.org/files/jpegsrc.v8d.tar.gz
Error: Could not download jpegsrc.v8d.tar.gz

  File "C:\Users\builder\MC_Main\Scripts\build-script.py", line 9, in <module>

issue_title: Miniconda macOS bash installer writes in .bash_profile instead of .profile
issue_body:
I've just installed miniconda on macOS Sierra and the installer **created** a `~/.bash_profile` file.

It is annoying as bash won't source anymore `~/.profile` when it finds `~/.bash_profile` !

For example, `brew` and `nvm` add their contents in `~/.profile`.

Instead of writing contents to `~/.bash_profile`, the installer should append its modifications to `~/.profile`.
Another option would be that miniconda installer adds a source command in `~/.bash_profile` when it creates it:
````bash
    [[ -r ~/.profile ]] && . ~/.profile
    [[ -r ~/.bashrc ]] && . ~/.bashrc
````

issue_title: replace path placeholder even if it's stored in a wide string
issue_body:
Prefix placeholder can be stored in binary files as a wide string (wchar_t).
In such a case it's not replaced by conda.
I came across this problem when building wxWidgets that is using internally wide strings: UCS-2 on Windows and UCS-4 on Unix:
```
007c4f10  2f 00 00 00 6f 00 00 00  70 00 00 00 74 00 00 00  |/...o...p...t...|
007c4f20  2f 00 00 00 63 00 00 00  6f 00 00 00 6e 00 00 00  |/...c...o...n...|
007c4f30  64 00 00 00 61 00 00 00  2f 00 00 00 63 00 00 00  |d...a.../...c...|
007c4f40  6f 00 00 00 6e 00 00 00  64 00 00 00 61 00 00 00  |o...n...d...a...|
007c4f50  2d 00 00 00 62 00 00 00  6c 00 00 00 64 00 00 00  |-...b...l...d...|
007c4f60  2f 00 00 00 77 00 00 00  78 00 00 00 77 00 00 00  |/...w...x...w...|
007c4f70  69 00 00 00 64 00 00 00  67 00 00 00 65 00 00 00  |i...d...g...e...|
```
Perhaps conda could try to replace the placeholder in any of the popular encodings (UTF8, UTF16, UTF32)?

issue_title: System-wide Anaconda installs on Windows are problematic
issue_body:
Copied from the Anaconda mailing list.  Suggests that we need to improve behavior for system-wide installs of Anaconda onto Windows systems.

---
**Comment from @msarahan:**

If you install system-wide, rather than user-local, then the installation is done with administrative rights.  Depending on where you put things, you might see permissions errors.

Unless you have good reason to want a system-wide install, user-local installs are much less likely to go wrong this way.

---
**Original post:**

During the installation of Anaconda 2.7, nothing indicated that it needed admin privileges to run.  However, virtually nothing runs without it.

If I open a powershell and try to run IPython, it crashes and the message is cryptic.  However, if I run IPython in a PowerShell as admin, it runs fine.  

I'm also having problems installing add-ons, which I assume is another admin issue.

Why is this necessary to run as admin??  Can this be fixed?

issue_title: How to specialize a package's runtime requirements during a build?
issue_body:
When a package has a dependency, but is very flexible about which version of that dependency is used, one would define the recipe's build and run requirements very generally. For example, the `hdf5` recipe needs `zlib`, but the version doesn't matter much. Therefore, https://github.com/conda/conda-recipes/blob/master/hdf5/meta.yaml simply specifies:
```
package:
  name: hdf5
  version: 1.8.14

requirements:
  build:
    - zlib
  run:
    - zlib
```
However, once hdf5 is built against a *specific* version of zlib (say, `zlib-1.2.8`), the run requirement must be restricted to zlib versions that are binary compatible with the one used during the build. This is indeed the case in official anaconda packages (the ones I get by calling `conda install hdf5`), whose `index.json` specifies
```
"depends": [
    "zlib 1.2*"
  ],
  "requires": [
    "zlib 1.2.8"
  ], 
```

However, I have not found any documented way to achieve an automatic specialization from `zlib` to `zlib 1.2*` in one's own recipes and builds. What am I missing?

issue_title: Breaking VariantRecord changes under 0.11.2.1
issue_body:
Hi,

Since updating to 0.11.2.1, I can no longer use the `VariantFile.new_record()` constructor for `VariantRecord`s. I receive the following error:

```
    std_rec = self.std_vcf.new_record()
  File "pysam/libcbcf.pyx", line 4282, in pysam.libcbcf.VariantFile.new_record (pysam/libcbcf.c:64486)
  File "pysam/libcbcf.pyx", line 2040, in pysam.libcbcf.VariantHeader.new_record (pysam/libcbcf.c:30838)
ValueError: At least one non-empty reference allele must be specified
```

The code works correctly under 0.11.

Additionally, it appears that reading from the END INFO field is no longer permitted.

```
    posB = record.info['END']
  File "pysam/libcbcf.pyx", line 2521, in pysam.libcbcf.VariantRecordInfo.__getitem__ (pysam/libcbcf.c:38363)
KeyError: 'END is a reserved attribute; access is via record.stop'
```

Finally, when I try to set a record's END under the new API, I cannot specify an END less than the start position. Could this requirement please be lifted? It prohibits interchromosomal breakend records where the END position is independent of the record's POS. 

```
> record.stop = 123
*** ValueError: Stop coordinate must be greater than or equal to start
```

Please let me know if I can supply additional information that would be helpful. Thanks for looking into this!

Matt

issue_title: TLAST not required on every axi_stream_slave transaction
issue_body:
Currently, every transaction of the axi_stream_slave requires that TLAST be set to '1', or else a check in the axi_stream_slave entity will fail, causing a failure of the VUnit test. However, this TLAST is not a requirement of the AXIS protocol and should only come on the last data beat of a packetized data stream.

As a workaround, I commented out the check in axi_stream_slave, as well as added a pop_axi_stream procedure (similar to the push_axi_stream procedure) that returns TLAST so that I can check/act on it myself.

issue_title: Disallow `master_only` if multiple hosts are used
issue_body: Multiple hosts is only for load balancing.  If `master_only` is used, it will break things.

issue_title: Suffix selection bug with trailing zeroes
issue_body:
It seems that there is an index selection problem in curator 4. 

Suffixes that end in `0` don't match.      In the following example, `value: -2016.30` will not match, but  `value: -2016.33` will.    As a hack, you can match the index ending in a zero using a regex: `value: (.*-2016.3[0])`

**1) Put some indices in ES:**

```
PUT /test-2016.30
PUT /test-2016.33
```

**2) create a curator snapshot action** 

```
actions:
  1:
    action: snapshot
    description:  Testing Suffix Issue
    filters:
    - filtertype: pattern
      kind: suffix
      value: -2016.30
      exclude:
```

**3) Do it**

```
├ $ curator --config localhost-config.yml --dry-run snapshot-suffix0-problem.yml  | grep remains
< nothing found >
```

It was also notice that the following value regex will match the index ending with a zero:

Hello,

Have had curator backing things up to s3 for almost 2 years but now going through some compliance revisions atm and investigating options for encrypting data.

issue_title: TransportError.__str__ fails, making exceptions unprintable
Need an ID to create a document
issue_body:
in version 5 it appears that a document ID must be specified to create a document. If one is not specified the following error appears:

    TypeError: create() missing 1 required positional argument: 'id'

This is nonsensical. If I do not specify an ID elasticsearch should create one for me.

issue_body:
#### Feature Request Description
Allow downloaded entities to have the same modified time as on blob or file storage.

#### Describe Preferred Solution
* Provide an option `--set-lmt` applicable only for the `download` operation.

LMT cannot be directly set on Azure unless through a different metadata field, which will not be implemented.

issue_title: [App Service] Support configuring traffic routing between slots

issue_title: Add VMSS public IP per VM feature to VMSS create
[Network] Allow auto-creating a public IP as part of creating a NIC
issue_body: The `az vm create` and `az network lb create` commands both allow auto-creating a referenced public IP if it doesn't already exist. This behavior is great, and it would be nice if the `az network nic create` command supported this as well, since it is the other resource type that can be logically associated with a public IP.

issue_title: [Generic Update] Not able to include equal sign (=) in the value field
issue_body:
A user complains that when using a generic update command to update service properties, warnings will be displayed if the value contains equal sign. Property update will be dropped.  You can refer to the following example.
```bash
>az iot hub update -n kevinzha-hub --set properties.storageEndpoints.$default.connectionString=Defau
ltEndpointsProtocol=https;AccountName=xxx;AccountKey=xxx

Property 'connection_string=_default_endpoints_protocol=https;_account_name=xxx;_account_key' not fo
und on $default. Update may be ignored.
```

The root cause of this issue is at [line 469 of arm.py](https://github.com/Azure/azure-cli/blob/master/src/azure-cli-core/azure/cli/core/commands/arm.py#L469). `rsplit` is used to parse the key and value. This means that value field can't include equal signs.
Is this by design? If not, the input key value expression shall be parsed more carefully.
I'd like to hear opinions from you guys.
Thanks very much!

issue_title: [DNS] 'az network dns zone list' - change columns in output
issue_body:
Currently, 'az network dns zone list' includes the following columns in the output, in the following order: etag, location, maxnumberofrecordsets, numberofrecordsets, resource group name

There are unnecessary items here (e.g. location is *always* global, hence not necessary). Etag is deep-dive information that is useful in the 'show' command but takes up too much space to justify inclusion in the zone list. And the order does not put the most important stuff first.

Please change to: zone name, resource group name, number of record sets, max number of record sets.

Also, to save space, I suggest renaming 'Max Number Of Record Sets' and 'Number Of Record Sets' to simply 'Max Record Sets' and 'Record Sets', respectively. If necessary, that is.

issue_title: Deleting deployment in a resource group doesn't delete the resources in the deployment
issue_body:
I opened this issue #585 regarding a while ago and we still feel strongly that resource_client.deployments.delete() should delete the resources under the deployment just as delete resource group does .  
We have a case where we have a vnet that contains a load balancer with multiple gateways attached to it. The load balancer and the gateway need to be within one resource group. So we have a resource group that contain both load balancer deployment and multiple gateway deployments.
Now if the user want to delete one gateway only, we try to delete deployment logic entity rather than delete the whole resource group which will delete both load balancer and all gateway deployments. T hen we are facing the fact that the resources under deployment are not deleted. Do we need to delete those resources one by one throughout deployment? If we can create a deployment in one template, why can't we delete the deployment template resources at one time just like resource group? I thought the delete deployment should bring in that function.

Thanks.
Li

issue_title: Failure to upload large file (>200GB)
issue_body:
Hi guys, 

I have been having issue with uploading large file (>200GB) with the library. According to the [documentation](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/storageservices/understanding-block-blobs--append-blobs--and-page-blobs), with the REST API version after 2016-05-31, the size limit can be up to 4.75TB (100 MB chunk x 50000 blocks). 
I have been playing with the chunk size limit (default is 4MB). When I changed the MAX_BLOCK_SIZE to be larger than 10MB, i saw ReadTimeout
`AzureException: ReadTimeout: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='host.blob.core.windows.net', port=443): Read timed out. (read timeout=20)`
When the block size was 10MB and `validate_content=True`, I got md5 mismatched 
`AzureHttpError: The MD5 value specified in the request did not match with the MD5 value calculated by the server.`
When I kept the block size the same (10MB) and disable md5 check, the file got uploaded successfully. I downloaded the file down and manually performed md5sum on the whole file, the md5 matched with the file on local drive. 
I updated the library version to 0.36.0, but it didn't make any difference. 

Could you please take a look if this is a bug/service limit, or if I did not do it correctly?

Thanks
